title: Event Handling Data Flow
subtitle: Detect → identify → update for every interaction
instructions: Select an item in the flowchart to the left to view the details of that step.
mermaid:
flowchart TD
    EventDetected[Event Detected]:::eventNode
    RegionIdentified[Region Identified]:::processNode
    DisplayUpdated[Display Updated]:::outputNode

    EventDetected --> RegionIdentified --> DisplayUpdated

    classDef eventNode fill:#1f77b4,stroke:#0a365c,stroke-width:3px,color:#ffffff,font-size:18px,font-weight:600
    classDef processNode fill:#f39c12,stroke:#8a5401,stroke-width:3px,color:#1f1f1f,font-size:18px,font-weight:600
    classDef outputNode fill:#2ca02c,stroke:#0f4120,stroke-width:3px,color:#ffffff,font-size:18px,font-weight:600
    linkStyle default stroke:#4d5c6b,stroke-width:3px

nodes.label: Event Detected
nodes.summary: Input hardware (mouse, touch, key, sensor) fires an event that the runtime captures.
nodes.details:
The operating system or browser normalizes a raw user gesture into an event with type, timestamp, and coordinates.

The infographic's listener subscribes to the relevant event channel (like pointerdown or keyup) and places the event into a queue so it can be handled deterministically.

Instrumentation hooks capture metadata so designers can observe how often the step is triggered.

nodes.label: Region Identified
nodes.summary: Hit-testing determines which interactive region or layer owns the event.
nodes.details:
The event dispatcher compares the event location with the registered regions for the infographic.

Spatial indexing and z-order rules resolve conflicts when multiple layers overlap.

Once ownership is decided, the sim fetches the region's state machine so it knows what transition should run next.

nodes.label: Display Updated
nodes.summary: State changes drive new visuals, instructions, and animations on screen.
nodes.details:
The owning region mutates its state (selection, hover, active path) and recomputes derived visuals.

Rendering hooks update SVG fills, text labels, and the explanatory infobox so students can immediately see the effect of their action.

Finally, the sim queues any follow-up events (timers, accessibility announcements) and waits for the next interaction.
